name: computer_control
description: Control the mouse, keyboard, and clipboard programmatically. Supports mouse movement, clicking, scrolling, keyboard key presses and hotkeys, typing text, and clipboard read/write. Requires pyautogui and pyperclip. FAILSAFE is enabled: moving the mouse to the top-left corner (0,0) aborts any operation.

amount.description: Number of scroll ticks for mouse_scroll. Default 3.

keys.description: Key(s) to press. Use '+' for simultaneous keys (e.g., 'ctrl+c'). For hotkey, use comma-separated keys (e.g., 'ctrl,alt,delete').

text.description: Text to type (type_text) or write to clipboard (clipboard_set).

duration.description: Mouse movement animation duration in seconds. Default 0.2.

interval.description: Delay between key presses or typed characters in seconds. Default 0.05.

relative.description: If true, mouse_move uses relative offset from current position. Default false.

presses.description: Number of times to repeat a key_press. Default 1.
